name: Krise Transportation Inc
owner_or_parent: American Student Transportation Partners (ASTP); ASTP being acquired by CVC DIF (infrastructure arm of CVC Capital Partners) from Access Holdings, announced Aug 2025
fleet_size_est: several hundred buses/vans (legacy Krise grew to ~500 pre-2004 sale; current footprint serves ~30 PA districts across 31 PA locations)
employees_est: hundreds (employee count of 13 in source data is materially understated; multi-terminal PA operator)
districts_or_customers: ~30 Pennsylvania school districts including Central Dauphin, Penn Hills, Hanover, Fleetwood, Bangor Area, Penncrest; integrated Brandywine ops in 2023
recent_news: 2021 partnered with ASTP (Access Holdings PE platform); 2023 absorbed Brandywine operations; Aug 2025 ASTP parent agreed to sale from Access Holdings to CVC DIF (DIF Infrastructure VII)
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Krise is a flagship portfolio company of ASTP, a PE-backed Northeast/Midwest school bus rollup now owned by CVC DIF. It is a direct competitor to Summit's own platform, not a target.

name: Everdriven
owner_or_parent: Charlesbank Capital Partners (acquired Mar 2024 from Palladium Equity Partners)
principals: Dean Holland (Board Member per source); management team built out under Palladium ownership
fleet_size_est: asset-light/marketplace model (no traditional fleet) using contracted Certified Driver Provider (CDP) network of small vehicles
employees_est: several hundred corporate (employee count of 6 in source data is wildly understated; nationwide platform)
service_types: alternative student transportation (small-vehicle SPED, McKinney-Vento homeless, foster, in-need)
districts_or_customers: ~700 school districts across 29 states; ~28,000 students annually; OMNIA Partners cooperative contract holder
recent_news: Mar 2024 Charlesbank Capital Partners acquired EverDriven from Palladium Equity Partners; under Palladium, built proprietary routing tech and national platform
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. EverDriven is a Charlesbank-owned national alternative-transportation platform (small vehicles/CDP model). It is a competitor/category-creator in the SPED and McKinney-Vento small-vehicle segment, not a regional family-owned operator.

name: On Time Transportation
owner_or_parent: independent (presumed; no parent or PE ownership identified)
fleet_size_est: micro (~12 employees per source data; likely a handful of vans)
districts_or_customers: Not identified in public sources; Sandy Springs / north Atlanta metro footprint (100 Camellia Ln, Atlanta, GA 30350)
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or growth news identified in web search
fit_notes: Low confidence. Generic name produces no public footprint connecting Ricky Aaron / 770-593-2008 to a confirmed school-bus or SPED contractor. Likely too small for a Summit tuck-in even if independent; recommend phone/LinkedIn diligence before pursuing.

name: Royal Limousine
fleet_size_est: micro (~12 employees per source data; small van/limo fleet)
service_types: special-needs (per source data tag); livery/limousine (per name)
districts_or_customers: Not identified in public sources; St. Louis metro (10677 Harriet Walk, Saint Louis, MO 63114 - Overland/St. Ann area)
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or growth news identified
fit_notes: Low confidence and likely a poor fit. Name suggests livery/black-car (distinct from school bus); the source data tag of 'special_needs=1' may reflect NEMT rather than SPED student transport. Not an obvious Summit School Services tuck-in; would need direct outreach to confirm whether they hold any district contracts.

name: Gentle Arms Daily Transportation
fleet_size_est: small (~12-20 vans; 28 employees per source data)
districts_or_customers: Listed on AgeSpan (Greater Lawrence/Merrimack Valley elder services) resource directory; serves elderly and disabled adults for medical appointments and adult day programs; no confirmed K-12 school district contracts
recent_news: No M&A, safety, or material news identified
fit_notes: Poor strategic fit. Gentle Arms is a small family-owned NEMT/elderly paratransit operator in Chelmsford MA, not a K-12 school-bus or SPED contractor. Founder Ann McGuigan is sole owner (potential succession over time) but the service mix is adjacent rather than core to Summit's school-bus thesis.
